Subject: Re: Your December Wedding in Tuscany

Dear CTX eval A,

Thank you so much—I'm thrilled you're moving forward!

I want to make sure we're aligned on a few details before you send the deposit this week.

**Deposit & Booking**  
Our standard retainer to secure your date is **50% of the total investment**, and it's non-refundable once the contract is signed. I don't want to confirm a different percentage without double-checking your specific contract terms, so let me verify that detail and send over the final agreement for your review—it will spell out the exact retainer amount and payment schedule.

**Engagement Session & Travel**  
I'd also like to clarify the engagement session logistics and any travel inclusions. Since your wedding is in Tuscany, let's confirm what's covered for pre-wedding sessions in the Florence area. I'll include those specifics in the contract so there's no ambiguity.

**Next Steps**  
I'll pull together your contract with all the confirmed details—retainer, timeline, travel, and deliverables—and send it over within the next business day. Once you've reviewed and we're aligned, you'll sign and submit the retainer, and your date will be officially held.

Does that work for you? I'm here if any questions come up in the meantime.

Warmly,  
[Studio Manager]  
[Studio Name]

We do carry general liability insurance as part of our standard business practice. However, the specifics of additional insured endorsements, coverage limits, and any associated fees can vary depending on the venue's requirements and our policy terms at the time of your wedding.

Rather than give you incomplete information, let me confirm the exact details with our operations team—including whether naming your venue as additional insured is already included, or if a rider and any related cost would apply. I'll have a complete answer for you within 24–48 hours.

In the meantime, if your venue has provided a specific certificate of insurance (COI) template or rider language, feel free to send that along—it will help us ensure everything aligns seamlessly.

Warm regards

whatEachLayerDoes.A: CRM snapshot only (weddings row) — no harness playbook_rules or case memories.
whatEachLayerDoes.B: + playbook_rules (same rows fetchActivePlaybookRulesForDecisionContext loads for live V3).
whatEachLayerDoes.C: + case memory (memories row type v3_verify_case_note) — appears in writer facts as case headers.
whatEachLayerDoes.D: + best-effort thread_summaries + prior messages on same thread before first wait (may race with worker).
whatEachLayerDoes.E: playbook without Elite row — failure-mode vs client saying Elite.

nextOptimization: If D’s continuity injection races, add a deterministic delay or a dedicated ‘pause until injected’ gate; if A still confirms Elite, tighten writer when playbook_rules blob is empty.
